title: Fxxxcking realitycheck!!!
author: FantasticShine4012
selftext: Ok guys  i have been backtesting crypto for months now like almost a year.  Downloaded tons of dukascopy data and als bought data from powakadata.  I tested my own strategy back and forth.  Then claude and chat came in the picture, provided me like hundreds of strategy, entry/exit logica’s.  Men i was busy for months.   Output…. Only losses..   so after hundreds of runs  claude finally told me today to stop with crypto.  Reason. It is only for the big hedgefunds boys.  So what now?????   Where are my fellow mates who do not give up!!???   What now

comments.author: sleeplong
comments.body: Its true, try stocks

replies.author: FantasticShine4012
replies.body: I need to change my whole mindset

replies.author: jaw_line
replies.body: lol I’m running into the same problem, it feels like I’m so close to grasping something worthwhile but breaking any sort of profitability barriers is no fkn joke fr

replies.author: FantasticShine4012
replies.body: So happy i am not the only one

replies.author: churchwa
replies.body: Said the same about FX to me

comments.author: melbkiwi
comments.body:
I have been using ChatGPT, Claude & GROK. 1st to write code and write outputs to csv, 2nd to check if the csv data has good clean outputs that it can trade from and the 3rd to check the 2nd id not trying to write code just validate data only. 
I’m only trading BTCUSD on MetaTrader4 at the moment.

comments.author: baconforbabies30
comments.body:
When you see an entry point based on historic data, flip a coin and let it decide if you go for the set up or opposite. 
Just kidding.

comments.author: No-Wrongdoer-9326
comments.body: Bro learn how the market work first you have to build your own model not rely on ai to build it for you. Ai just give you the framework you have to backtest by yourself. Consider adding layer 2 data analytic platform to read liquidity like bookmap or quantflows or atas

replies.author: FantasticShine4012
replies.body: I will definitely look into this. Thanks man

comments.author: WhatKatieDid2
comments.body: For trading in general, gold is the one. It has loads of really beautiful recurring patterns on the five minute chart.

comments.author: Think_Soft2282
comments.body: Claude gave you the best financial advice you'll ever get for free. AI can only back test historical patterns based on logic you give it, but crypto retail markets are dominated by institutional algos, order-flow manipulation, and funding rate traps. If an LLM could code a winning strategy in a afternoon, everyone would be a millionaire. The fact that you did hundreds of runs and only found losses means your back testing engine is working perfectly; it saved you from losing real money.

comments.author: Whakomatic
comments.body: Just because it was hot this time last year does not mean it will be hot tomorrow. That’s the problem with backtesting.

comments.author: Star-Trading
comments.body: Try papertrading or stocks

comments.author: Ready_Log4016
comments.body: It just probly means the strat has no edge, or only works in certain market conditions. Most AI-generated strats die once you add fees, slippage, and chop. I'd stop random systems and focus on one idea at a time. I backtest on blueblocx to see flows and on-chain context, not just price patterns. Don't quit, but don't force a broken strat either.

comments.author: FetchBI
comments.body:
This is what makes me profitable, it’s an approach almost no one has heard of:

Gamma Exposure Level calculations on the options chain > translate the reference prices to Futures > translate those levels to CFD indices levels > trade the hedging levels on hit

I’ve made a video about it: 
https://youtu.be/Kf0vLH-9O44?is=MGkKxJHQJnvK9tOL

replies.author: FantasticShine4012
replies.body: Interesting man.  I will definitely check this out and will run my powakadata on it.

comments.body: [removed]

comments.author: FilmFreak1082
comments.body: My Claude helped me build one that works - generated about 10% profits per month since Feb (bad market). Getioi.app if you're curious -> built my own algo from scratch - trades only on binance.com

comments.author: yashBoii4958
comments.body: Astop optimizing entries and test regime filters instead most retail crypto algos die because they ignore volatility regimes entirely.i switched to markets xyz for the leaderboard transparency honestly

comments.author: Egp006
comments.body: Tienes que usar strategy quant es un programa donde haces estrategias y las validas.

comments.author: kingdomstrategies
comments.body: this has been my experience with creating indicators, nothing passes on backtests, Im sure our platforms are different but your build strategy seems similar to mines, this is what I've done, I've built indicators first based on deep research and revised and planned via claude, once I'm happy with the indicators THEN I backtest the indicator, is a huge difference, I' built about  30 indicators, all backtested and working on manual and semi manual entries, Then you build the full treategy, absolutely nothing will pass on backtests.

replies.author: Scared_Pepper_1701
replies.body: Care to share what kind of indicators you're making?

comments.author: Ok-Caterpillar-8992
comments.body: Don't listen to the 'crypto is only for hedge funds' crowd. Although I started with portfolio optimization with stocks, learning the fundamentals, I moved into crypto Took time, took losses, took iteration. Now I'm profitable in both. The logs are in my profile if you want to see.

comments.author: Objective-Part-2346
comments.body: you are 100% right with AI. However I think that speed of progress will slow down. We're going to reach a plateau at some point in the coming months. So my advise will be more "wait & see". I am not saying stay inactive. Take this phase as an opportunity to learn how to use AI tools and coding.

comments.author: jakesully84
comments.body: Was trying for months . To train an agent. No luck. No matter what I do , still the agent can't figure out the trades. Biggest problem I have it they exit the trade after two or three steps. Mean 30 or 45 minutes in 15 minutes timeframe data

comments.author: Sharpe_Engineer
comments.body:
Im using claudecode and backtesting on NQ after doing 2 versions of tqqq and one of qqq5 over last 27.2 years. Not only did they durvive hard years - they thrived immensely. 
Now improving everything and having flexible leverage..im building on NQ and its gonna be much more great and also done right..a big system..not 1 simple strategy.
And the results of my qqq5 and what i can project for my NQ already..their absolutely insane..you wont ever ever believe me.

So i gotta say you have to learn and direct it. Dont use ai in the trading itself yea? Be sure to abide by safety protocols and never ever play with that, cause if you do youll get a false image of success.

My bottom line is that a lot is possible, but you have to learn and you have to work *with* the ai and not just throw at it research this and make me this.

What im personally building seem to be very exceptional..time will tell tho, its very hard and challenging but this is because im not going for one cute strategy..im going for the best i can possibly make. If i can believe that ill get even a litltle fraction of that in reality it would be great.

As for crypto, i havent done BTC yet or crypto, but im 100% suree theres plenty of potential there. Plenty.
I will somwhen get to bitcoin, and maybe somewhen even later get to a few of the largest coins too, just because i care for capacity. You can make hige percentages with small coins if youll work smart and *test* your ideas with data and abide safety protocols, always.

comments.body: Gosh AI trading just doesn’t work does it
